title: Implement AVX-512 IFMA kernels for prime field batch operations
description:
Implement AVX-512 IFMA (Integer Fused Multiply-Add) kernels for GF(p) batch arithmetic using `VPMADD52HUQ`/`VPMADD52LUQ` instructions. These instructions perform `(a × b) + c` on packed 52-bit integers, enabling 8 parallel field multiplications per instruction.

## Background

AVX-512 IFMA (available on Intel Ice Lake+ and AMD Zen 4+) provides fused multiply-add on 52-bit integer operands packed in 512-bit registers. This is the optimal instruction for modular arithmetic on primes that fit in 52 bits, which covers the vast majority of practical prime fields (p < 2^52).

## Target instructions

| Instruction | Operation | Throughput |
|-------------|-----------|------------|
| `VPMADD52LUQ` | Low half: `(a[51:0] × b[51:0])[51:0] + c` | 8 parallel ops/instruction |
| `VPMADD52HUQ` | High half: `(a[51:0] × b[51:0])[103:52] + c` | 8 parallel ops/instruction |

Together, these compute an exact 104-bit product + 64-bit accumulator for 8 independent field elements simultaneously.

## Kernel operations

### Batch Montgomery multiplication
```rust
/// Multiply 8 GF(p) elements in parallel using IFMA + Montgomery reduction.
/// Inputs and outputs are in Montgomery form.
pub unsafe fn batch_mul_mont_ifma(
    a: &[u64; 8],
    b: &[u64; 8],
    p: u64,
    p_inv: u64,
) -> [u64; 8];
```

### Batch modular addition/subtraction
```rust
/// Branchless modular addition of 8 elements.
/// result = (a + b) - P if (a + b) >= P, else (a + b)
/// Uses AVX-512 mask comparison to avoid branches.
pub unsafe fn batch_add_mod_ifma(a: &[u64; 8], b: &[u64; 8], p: u64) -> [u64; 8];
```

### Batch dot product with delayed reduction
```rust
/// Compute dot product of two FieldVec slices using IFMA.
/// Accumulates multiply-adds in wide (104-bit) registers, reducing
/// only when overflow is imminent (kmax scheduling).
pub unsafe fn batch_dot_product_ifma(
    a: &[u64],
    b: &[u64],
    p: u64,
    p_inv: u64,
    kmax: usize,
) -> u64;
```

## Limb decomposition for p > 52 bits

For primes 52 < p < 64 bits, a single IFMA multiply doesn't suffice. Two approaches:

1. **Two-limb decomposition**: Split each 64-bit element into two 52-bit limbs. Multiplication becomes 3 IFMA instructions (Karatsuba on the limbs) + reduction.
2. **Fallback to scalar Montgomery**: For primes near 2^64, scalar may be faster than the limb overhead.

Benchmark both and select via the runtime dispatch mechanism.

## Location

- Kernels: `crates/gf2-kernels-simd/src/x86/ifma.rs` (new file)
- Safe wrappers: Integration with `FieldBackend` dispatch in `gf2-core`

## Dependencies

- 4b5d8948 (AVX-512 SIMD backend) — the AVX-512 infrastructure and runtime detection must exist
- 8ce6f8aa (Montgomery multiplication for GF(p)) — scalar Montgomery must exist as the reference implementation and fallback

## Success criteria

- Batch multiplication produces identical results to scalar Montgomery for all tested primes.
- Throughput: ≥4× improvement over scalar Montgomery for Fp<65537> batch multiplications.
- Dot product: within 2× of fflas-ffpack's `fdot` for equivalent prime field sizes.
- Runtime detection: gracefully falls back to scalar on CPUs without IFMA support.
